> We systematically study Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) across math, coding, and vision benchmarks and uncover that RL fine-tuning enhances sampling efficiency without expanding the reasoning capacity already present in base models.

limit-of-rlvr.github.io

> we find that [RLVR] does not elicit fundamentally new reasoning patterns... observed reasoning abilities originate from and are bounded by the base model... By contrast, we find that distillation can introduce new reasoning patterns... and genuinely expand the model's reasoning capabilities

posts.text:
Here's #SCOTUS summarily reversing the three-judge district court's ruling in the Texas redistricting case *because* of its "interim" grant of emergency relief back in December.

That's not surprising, of course. But it's yet another data point for why calling it the "interim" docket is misleading.

SCOTUS summarily reverses the lower court order striking down TX’s Republican gerrymander that SCOTUS let go into effect back in December on the shadow docket. 

IOW, SCOTUS reversed a case on the merits with no reasoning other than saying they were bound by two paragraphs on the shadow docket.
